and have full access to bonus content, that's an option.
To find out how, please go to dogmanencounters.com forward slash podcast.
One of tonight's guests, Kai Roth, was featured on episode 630 last week
where he came on and talked about a dogman he had some encounters with in South Africa
that he calls White Mane.
Well, he agreed to come back and do a Q&A because we didn't have nearly enough time
for me to ask all the questions I had for him.
on that show. Well, as an added surprise, I found out in the process of scheduling this
Q&A that Marcus had an interest in joining us, Marcus Smit. So for tonight's show, I
want to welcome both Kai Roth and Marcus Smit back on. Gentlemen, thanks so much for joining
us.
Thank you for having us, Vic.
Thank you. It's a big pleasure to be here.
Well, it's great having you. Kai, for the people who missed episode 630, please
give us a brief bio on yourself. Yeah, my name is Kairos. I'm 19 years old. I've been born and
raised in Cape Town my whole life. I moved around quite a bit here and there. And right now I
currently live in a small holiday village called Pringle Bay where I've had my sightings with
white man. Yeah, I mean, I'm a very outdoors person. I love nature, always have, always loved
animals my whole life. And yeah, I'd love being in the ocean. I've got a good group of mates. I
can't complain about that. And yeah, I'm out of school. I matriculated two years ago and I'm
currently working. And yeah, that's pretty much about me. All right. How about you, Marcus?
Yeah, my name is Marcus. I met Kai here in this little town. I moved in about two years before him.
And me and my family also moved around in South Africa a lot.
And we spend almost every end of the year camping out at a campsite.
So we're also a very outdoors family.
The friend group that me and Kai are part of, we love going on camps as well.
At one point we did a few regular scheduled camps, which was very fun.
Um, like I also love animals and nature and he's been getting me into the surfing and
body boarding that we do here at the local beach.
Both of you guys sound so nice.
It sounds to me like you're awfully lucky to have each other as friends and the fact
that you live in such a beautiful place.
I mean, it doesn't get any better than that.
Yeah, I would agree.
I would agree strongly.
Yeah, it sure doesn't.
Kai, can you give us a brief flyover of those encounters you told us about with white man on episode 630 for the people who might have missed that episode?
Yeah, of course, Nick.
So in summary, what happened was about a week.
I had a very off feeling that something was watching me at night.
It was specifically strong at night and I would be very on edge.
I'd have my eyes darting around, my ears would be tuned in, and the hair on my body would be raised.
And then shortly after I had a sighting with a dog man we believe to be a dog man which we later called white man
And in summary, what happened was my dog was barking at night and I went out with my sister to go get the dog.
And I noticed there was something very off with the dog, her aggression level and the hair on her back.
and then I saw what I first I thought was an old neighbor walking in the road and when I
greeted them the said person dropped down to all fours and me and this dog man were kind of in a
stalemate position for about five to ten minutes whilst my sister got my dad and my dad came out
and then my dad switched on the car and a dogman booked it down the empty plot across our road
and um yeah i went to bed that night and at about 11 o'clock it came back and was tapping on our
outside window um and after about an hour of it i got full of what was happening and looked outside
and I saw its silhouette standing literally two panes of glass away.
And then I got my parents, I got my dad,
and we went outside and couldn't find anything
and nothing else for the rest of that night.
And then, yeah, I mean, that's the only sightings I've ever had.
I obviously ran through some of my friends' sightings
and encounters in the last episode.
um and then yeah pretty much just me feeling off for a few weeks and months after that but that's
pretty much what i experienced um yeah and i told about in the last episode did a great job sharing
those experiences too marcus on episode 630 of course kai told us about your encounter but
it's a lot different to have someone share your experience than to share it yourself
So now I want you to please tell us in your own words, what happened when you had that
experience?
Absolutely.
So about one or two months, maybe a few weeks more than that, um, off the kite sighting
of the dog, man, uh, it was an overcast weather with a bit of a drizzle coming down.
I decided that I was going to go on a walk.
It's one of my favorite types of weather to go for a walk in.
And I decided I'm going to head off into our local forest.
And so I took my skateboard with me and the entrance of the forest,
I hid it under a bush and I walked in.
Now, as I'm walking in, usually in this type of, when the area gets wet,
you have lots of these little frogs croaking, but in their hundreds croaking.
So there's constantly this noise going on.
And I hear some birds chirping around.
And so to get to the specific area I was planning on going to, you have to walk through this
almost tunnel of these long, sticky trees.
And so I'm making my way through the tunnel.
And for some other reason, the hairs on my neck start to raise.
And so at the time, I didn't think much of it.
I think I told myself it's probably just the eagerness of it all.
and it pushed through. So I walked through the tunnel and I get up on this sand dune. And so
this is now this sort of open plain with the fane was growing at about your knees length and it's
just this vast open plain with the road about a few hundred meters off. And so there's a little
footpath you follow and then you break off. I broke off from the footpath still just pushing
through whatever I was feeling at the time. And I, you round this one. So I'm planning on going to
this river valley. There's the lagoon river that comes all the way down from the mountains and
this place I'm going to has a lovely rock that I was planning on sitting by and just carving sticks
and whatnot. To get there, you have to round this bush and then there's a drop down, maybe about
two meters, but you then just slide down and then you end up on the rock. So as I then round bush,
I noticed that everything just went dead silent. All the frogs, all the birds, there was nothing.
And that immediately put me off because I know that if there's something, if there's a predator
animal around, usually the other animals go quiet.

And so I immediately jumped at the thought that maybe there a leopard in the area We have baboons but they don really cause such a reaction in the other animals So I immediately thought there was a leopard nearby And so leopards you can scare away quite easily
if you know how it's very, you just act very intimidating, don't lose your ground,
and they'll run off. And they're very shy in the first place as well.
So I just decided to keep on going. And as I stepped in this, again, there's this little bowl
behind this bush that leads to the entrance of the rock beach. I look over across the river
and I see this white object hidden behind lots of foliage. And my first thought was that it's
just some of the foam. When the lagoon water gets stale, it produces this white foam. So my first
thought was it's probably just a heap of foam behind all the foliage. As I take maybe three
steps into that little bowl, I see this white mass rise up, take a few steps, and I'm immediately
frozen. It takes a few steps away from me, stops, and then keeps walking upstream. And
once I lose sight of it, I just immediately, I crouch down, I hunker up, I take out my pocket
knife for what it was worth and i just very slowly and very quietly backed out all the way that
entire route i was walking backwards just keeping my eye on the area from which i came and i remember
i got to the exit of the forest grabbed my skateboard and i bolted down and maybe
500 meters past the forest i started hearing the frogs again and i remember there seeing that thing
just the hairs everywhere raised very similar to what i described just all over my body is
releasing i remember feeling a strange dread but i remembered i'm just like i'm just gonna
crouch down i'm just gonna hide seeing it walk away from me i saw it obviously didn't want
anything to do with me and i remembered it's just stay quiet i'm just gonna leave not gonna
look for trouble whatever this thing is it just looks like it wants to mind it
and that's about as much as i can remember i remember just as now after the entire encounter
i remember i stayed boarded back home went locked myself in the room and i just sat for a bit
maybe an hour or two just to process the entire thing and afterwards i told kai and the rest of
the friends what happened that's a lot to take in did you ever have any other experiences marcus
that you could attribute to white main where maybe you think he might have been tormenting you after
that first sighting just the same way that he did with kai after kai had his first encounter with
him so off the top of my head i don't really remember any significant sightings like that one
after that i do remember a few nights after that the dogs were very we have at the time we had two
dogs they were extremely on edge the whole night long i remember my dad made a point of it the next
morning to tell me that he just couldn't get any sleep letting the dogs in and then they want to
come out and then they want to go they're just in and out the house the entire night long and i
remember as well that night just hearing the dogs barking and howling but that's as close as i can
think to a direct connection to white mane oh good i'm glad he didn't foresee a shoe and torment you
then hate the fact that he did that to kai though if you've had a dogman encounter that you need
help dealing with or if you'd like to be a guest on the show, please go to dogmanencounters.com
and submit a report. If you've had a Bigfoot sighting or would like to be a guest on one
of my two Bigfoot podcasts, please go to mybigfootsighting.com. I know you two are good
friends, very good friends, but before you actually laid eyes on White Mane,
how sure were you that Kai actually had experienced what he told you he had?
Did you believe him 100% or was there a little bit of doubt there?
When Kai first told me, I remember me and my friends kind of made a joke about it.
But he just never caved in.
Like he didn't go, oh, it's all a joke.
I literally just kept pressing us and telling us that what he saw was real.
And I think for the whole day long, he's just telling us the same thing over and over and over.
and I realized that, okay, he probably did something. My first
thought was that it was maybe someone dressed up as something
or maybe someone who painted themselves white, or maybe it's

just someone playing a prank And I sort of that was one of the doubts i had until i went and had and saw there the thing at the lagoon and it was at that point that i just realized whatever kai told me
described or what he saw that's probably the thing i saw this might be hard to admit but in a way
were you hoping maybe that kai had just mistook something like you just laid out for us and hadn't
actually seen what he said he saw? Because Pringle Bay sounds like a beautiful place,
and it's obvious that you two have an amazing time running around doing all the things you do
together. Well, when you have a dogman running around Paradise, that tends to change things.
That takes some of the shine off of a very pretty thing like Pringle Bay. Deep down,
Were you hoping that maybe he had mistakenly identified something else as being the dog man?
So at first I was more scared that we're dealing with someone who's lost their mind, who has just gone insane.
Because if a person is acting like that, then there's really something going on.
But after my encounter with it, I don't really feel threatened by it.
I feel the way that I managed to react to it and how it just went off on its own.
I feel like it might just be another animal that wants to be left alone.
I've had this thought come up that it does make walking around alone, which is something I love to do,
it makes that a bit more difficult for me.
but like i said i haven't had any other um horrible encounters with it or any other encounters
matter of fact at all with it and so i feel like i almost see it as just part of the wildlife
oh that's a really good thing i'm glad you're able to deal with this in such a healthy way that sure
does be the alternative kai white mane tormented you like i said earlier after you had that first
encounter with him do you see that as an indication that he's an evil being or do you think that's
just how this particular dogman is he was just trying to get some jollies by tormenting you the
way he did um to be completely honest with you vick um in my mind after thinking about it for
quite a while I feel like that tormenting afterwards was in my mind a bit more of curiosity
from its side I think it was just trying to test me and some part of it may have been trying to
scare me for the fun of it but in my mind I've I've always viewed it as it just being like curious
about me um but to be honest with you again I don't live in fear of it every day and I to be
honest with you, I'm on the same boat as Marcus. I feel as though it is just a part of the wildlife.
And unfortunately, that is what the situation is at the moment. Whether we like it or not,
it lives here. And as I said in the last episode, I do believe that it is a flesh and blood creature.
So, I mean, whether we like it or not, in my eyes and in my opinion, I view it as a part of
the wildlife and the ecosystem here.
I really do like the outlook that both of you guys have.
That's really good.
Kai, when you're in that standoff with him, the first time you saw him, could
you see any expression on his face?
Um, and so to be honest with you, I couldn't see any like human expressions
on its face, if that's what you're asking.
So it didn't give me a smile or a frown or anything like that.
It more just looked shocked because it had these perfectly circular eyes and it looked like it had them open as wide as possible.
And its ears were facing me, but they didn't twitch once.
So if I'm being honest with you, the only feeling I got off of white men when I had my first standoff with it was that it was defensive and it was a bit surprised.
So it was kind of stood there just waiting to see what I was doing or what I was going to do.
So to answer your question, I would say shocked is the only thing I can really, the only word I can really give you.
But it didn't, it didn't like physically give me a human emotion or like a, like a gesture on its face.
Due to his positioning or mannerisms, you said he clearly seemed to be shocked, but did he ever do anything that gave you the impression he might have been afraid?
I don't think it was afraid at any point.
I think it was just defensive.
Like, how I saw it is it knew its power.
It knew what it was capable of, and it knew it had the upper hand.
It was just waiting to see what my play was.
Obviously, I'm thankful that I didn't do anything else.
But say, for example, maybe I moved forward, and it knew it had the upper hand,
and it felt defensive.
It could have either come at me or ran away.
So I wouldn't say it was scared at any point.
I think it was just testing me in a way.
I obviously think, again, my dog was going and probably it noticed the dog, but didn't give much notice to it.
And then when I came, I maybe scared it a bit or gave it a bit of a fright and so immediately jumped into defensive and then was just over time watching to see what I would do.
So that would be my answer to you.
I don't think it was scared.
I think it was more just assessing me and seeing what I was like and what I would do.
Well, that's good.
You noticed all that.
You're probably spot on.
That's probably what was going on.
Marcus, when you saw White Mane that day at the Lagoon River, it sounds to me like he might have been hiding from you before he eventually rose, making it very easy for you to see him.
Do you think he actually was hiding from you at first?
I'm not really sure about that.
My impression was that it was almost like certain animals, they graze.
I feel like white mane at that point was really just minding its own business.
It wasn't laying down or hiding.
I know that for a fact.
It was definitely upright, but I think it was on all fours.
It was when I went closer and saw it that it rose and walked off.
The way I took it was that I sort of intruded on this animal's maybe territory or maybe its personal space, and it just didn't like the feel of that, and it just walked off.
And it walking off to me was sort of, that was the chance I took to just leave it alone.
I'm not going to mess with this creature.
But back to your question, I don't think it was hiding.
I think it was just minding its own business.
If you think he was put off by you being there that day, as far as his posture would go, how would you describe it?
Was he more hunched forward or more upright?
So when I first saw it, thinking about its physiology now, it was most likely crouched down on all fours.
And it was only that I got close to it that it then reared up.
But it definitely had forward arching back.
with very long, lanky legs.
And I would assume, by the way, it was walking that it had a backward-bending knee
that you'd see in dogs.
Those are good descriptions.
Kai, baboons don't come down to the ground at night.
Do you go on your night walks instead of day walks
to avoid the ones that run around your village or for some other reason?
To be honest with you, Vic, we probably walk around more during the day
than we do at night.
and the baboons here don't bother you unless if you have food on you so I mean we walk around
during the day more than we do at night the main reason why we go out at night is purely because
it's more quiet there's no cars going around there's not people talking and it really gives
you an opportunity to just accept the peace of the place of this town that we live in
and I suppose it's just always that thing of being out at night.
Like, I don't know, it just kind of lifts our mood a bit
and it helps everyone to interact with each other better
and to have a better time than if we were to do something during the day.
And, yeah, I guess we all just enjoy exploring at night.
It's just because then we have the cover of dark
so we can go into these empty, abandoned housings
or plots and go explore these places without having some grumpy old lady on our rear ends
calling someone because they want to make a complaint.
So, yeah, I reckon we walk at night a lot less than what we do during the day.
But the reason we go at night is just to have a bit of a better experience.
That makes sense.
Yeah, if I didn't ask you that question, it'd be pretty easy to jump to conclusions on why
you did that, bud.
I'm glad we got it straight from the horse's mouth.

Kai when you first saw White Mane what kind of substrate was he standing on You said he was about 20 meters away from you Was it grass dirt or something else So it was a gravel road so it a hard grounding underneath with a bunch of loose rock
and then it ran away onto grass, which is probably like half a meter, and then jumped into this
marsh like reeds
and it's muddy and it's wet
and he
pretty much booked it along there
but those are pretty much
the only terrains I would say
really went across. It was standing
on the gravel when I had to stand
off with it and then it ran across
the
marshland but
the problem with that
marshland is a lot of it is sewage and oil
and this and that from neighbouring houses
that have their irrigations flow out in there.
Listening to you describe how things were laid out,
obviously on that hard, gravelly ground across the road,
you're not going to find any prints.
But if he did move off into softer ground the way you said he did,
did you ever go over there looking for prints?
No, we did not go looking for prints.
The one thing that we were mainly focused on, myself and Marcus,
was calculating its speed.
because I remember we went back about a few days after I had my sighting
and we took an estimate of how long the plot was
and I gave a time and we calculated it.
And I won't say the exact speed because I can't recall it,
but we came to a conclusion that it was something unbelievably fast
and something much faster than any animal we have here in our biosphere.
But to answer your question, Vic, no,
we didn't look for tracks purely because it is unbelievably difficult to move in.
And it just, that marshland stinks of poo and oil.
And it's just really not a place you want to go in unless you really have to.
That's totally understandable.
When he went into what seemed to be that defensive mode across the road from you,
was he looking right at you the whole time or was he looking in a different direction?
Oh no, his eyes were locked with mine.
We were just, he didn't move his eyes once.
When I was looking at him and assessing the other details of his body,
I didn't break eye contact with him.
I was more using the perimeter of my vision to assess that.
But I never, my eyes always stayed on his and his stayed on mine.
And we didn't break eye contact up until the point that my dad turned on the engine
and he ran down the plot.
When he bolted into that marsh after your dad started his car, did he do so on two legs or four legs?
He ran on four legs, but from what I saw, it looked like a very weird run.
It looked like almost he was grabbing the ground and then throwing himself forward.
It didn't look like a normal dog's run.
And that's another thing that caught me was it looked like he would grab with one hand, pull, and then grab with the next and pull.
And just almost ran like that.
It didn't look like a dog.
I physically saw the elbows bend and still hold the ground up until he fully extended his arm again.
And so, yeah, that's pretty much how I would describe that he moved.
Your head must have been spinning at the time, and understandably so.
So if you don't know the answer to this, it's totally understandable.
But by chance, did you ever notice if he put his back legs between his front legs when he would run and pull back like that, or if they went outside of his front legs?
um yeah if i recall correctly his front legs came so he it was almost like he was hovering so it was
like his front legs would come in between and then he would he would play not like barely very
lightly place his back legs and then almost throw his front arm his front legs over again and then
grab um because the movement was i would see it speed up and then slow down and speed up and slow
down but it did so unbelievably fast it was literally like a fraction of a second that he
paused and then he'd like speed up again in a fraction of a second that he paused and speed up
again if i'm understanding you it sounds like what you witnessed was him pulling himself forward to

propel himself and his back legs were almost like an afterthought kind of just supporting his weight Is that accurate Yes that how I would describe it And my guess behind the reasoning for that was purely because of the terrain that he was running
And my guess would be that it was like he was grabbing onto the roots or the stems of the reeds and pulling himself forward.
Because the water in that marshland is unbelievably thick.
and then it falls into like different layers of mud.
And it's, as I said before, it is extremely hard to move in.
I've tried it once and it is just, you try to pull your foot up
and you just get like, it vacuum seals your foot into the mud.
So I personally think he was trying to put as little pressure
on the mud as possible and was trying to use the surroundings to move.
The reason why I asked about that is quadrupeds.
they almost always use their back legs for propulsion and their front legs catch their
weight. Their front legs do provide some propulsion, but mostly the front legs are
just to catch their weight when they're in full run. For him to do the opposite, that is really
strange. But then again, dogmen do a lot of things that just fly in the face of what's normal. So
that's just one of those things. But yeah, that's why I was asking you about that.
Marcus, have you ever heard about someone besides Kai and your other friends having an experience with something that just might have been white mean?
No, I haven't really heard anything specifically about white.
That me and my friends have, but I have heard other people mentioned that they hear strange sounds outside the house.
or that some people, it sounded like someone was,
an animal, a quadruped animal specifically,
was prowling around the house,
but nothing that can concretely tell me it was white-maned.
Well, I'm glad you're not jumping to conclusions.
That's good.
Marcus, is there any novelty to the fact that at any time when you go out,
you might bump into him again,
Or would you be totally fine if he disappeared from the face of the earth and never came back and that was never even a possibility?
No, I'm quite excited about the fact that I could see him again.
Like I said, it wasn't a negative experience that I had.
Sometimes I sort of hope when I'm walking around at night specifically,
as I hope I sometimes just maybe catch a glimpse of something,
maybe just bump into him, well not physically, but just see him across the road,
walking back into the mountains or something.
I really do. It would be a cool experience for me to see.
Obviously, I'd be a bit frozen in fear, very cautious on my way home.
But if he's gone, I would be just a little bit bummed out about that.
Well, it's good to hear you say those things, because if you have that desire to see him again sometime, then you can't be that bad off after having that encounter at the Lagoon River.
And speaking of that place at the Lagoon River, how far is that from your home?
Shoot, I'd say maybe.
Five kilometers, I would if I guessed.
Um, it's about maybe four kilometers on dirt roads and tar roads, and then
another kilometer into the wilderness.
It's not that far then.
Is there much at all in the way of infrastructure between that place in
your home houses, other buildings, businesses, or you talked about the dirt
roads and everything is a nothing but bush.
so between the forest and my residence there is roads and houses and some shops it's it's quite um
i live in very central pringle bay well if you didn't want to have any visits late at night i
guess that might help but as i'm sure you know these things do come into towns so it definitely
doesn't preclude the chance kai when you were laying in bed that night and heard that tapping
on your bedroom window. It doesn't seem like the tapping sounded like claws on glass,
considering how you described it. Did it sound like it might have been knuckles instead?
To be 100% honest with you, Vic, I haven't the slightest clue. I don't know if it was
knocking on my window. I don't know if it was tapping. All I know is that when I opened up my
curtain, I saw the hand out with the index finger extended and the thumb slightly
out of the hand.
Um now again whether it had its left hand and had it backwards or if it had its right hand facing forwards I really don know
But, yeah.
I'm sure you're so frightened that you're doing good to know your own name.
So you can't be faulted for not knowing exactly what it sounded like when you heard that tapping.
So I understand.
Kai, after your dad pulled his car around to point his headlights where Whitemane was
when you first saw him, you told your dad that you had seen the dogman.
How did your dad know back then what a dogman was?
To be honest with you, Vic, I'm pretty sure he just made a guess of the two words together
and probably assumed that it was some sort of mix of a creature
mixed with a man and a dog.
I don't think he had the full rundown or idea of what it was.
My dad's never really been into that kind of stuff.
He's a very religious man.
And so, I mean, to be honest with you,
he never asked me what a dog man was.
I didn't tell him it was a dog man.
I just described to him what I'd seen, purely because I was still in shock and I didn't know
what, I didn't want to put a name on it yet. So I just described to him what I saw,
this white creature with a tail that I thought was our neighbor walking at first and then dropped
down and it had these pointy ears with these black sunken eyes. And he pretty much just told me like,
don't talk about it purely for the sake of my younger sisters.
I'm glad you brought that up.
Let's talk more about that.
When you had that experience, like you said, he told you not to bring it up with your sister or any other family members for that point.
Down the road, did you ever tell that sister who was with you that night what it was that you saw what happened?
Or have you to this day kept that from her?
So funny enough, I never told her personally.
I mean she asked me questions when I got inside and I said I don't know that's literally the only
response I had at the time she was asking what was it what do you think it is and I was just
saying I don't know I don't know and then when you released the last episode last week my family
actually listened to it and my eldest sister who's turning 16 this year the one who was with
me for that encounter listened um and then the two youngest stayed um well they went to bed
so now she knows of my encounter in detail oh good it's good that she knows but if she does
know in detail about what happened to you and about white mane being out there
have you noticed any changes in her behavior um to be honest with you no vick so she
she lives with her mom about two hours outside of Pringle Bay towards Cape Town so we only get to
see her every second weekend but I mean I got home on Saturday from Johannesburg and
she was quite eager and she was asking like can we go for a night walk tonight can you ask dad
if we can go for a night walk um so I don't think it's I don't think it's um I suppose you'd say
caused any hesitation on her side um i do i do think and hope that she believes me but i don't
